cards.front: What is the primary function of the Domain Name System (DNS)?
cards.back: To translate domain names to IP addresses that computers can understand

cards.front: What is the purpose of a Name Service?
cards.back: To manage and resolve names in a distributed system

cards.front: What is the main advantage of using DNS over IP addresses?
cards.back: It makes it easier to manage and resolve names, rather than memorizing IP addresses

cards.front: How does DNS work in a distributed system?
cards.back: DNS requests are sent to a recursive resolver, which queries a root DNS server, and then a domain name server, to resolve the name to an IP address

cards.front: What type of DNS server is responsible for resolving names at the top-level of the DNS hierarchy?
